title: ceropegia urceolata
intro: Ceropegia urceolata, also known as the helmet flower or Indian squill, is a flowering plant species in the milkweed family (Apocynaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Ceropegia urceolata are believed to include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of various skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by drying and processing the leaves, roots, and flowers, which can be used to create teas, infusions, and topical creams.
